frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: Arre Priya, family function mein saree ya achha sa salwar pehen lo beta, usmein ladki zyada decent lagti hai. Yeh western kapde theek hain bahar ke liye, par ghar ke bade-buzurgon ke saamne thoda maryada mein rehna achha lagta hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it links a woman's respectability and decency to traditional clothing, implying western clothes are less appropriate or respectable. It encodes dress policing in a family-event context using elegance/decency framing and generational voice.
original_frame: Marital Rape: A Husband's Right, A Wife's Duty
persona_text: You are Deepa. Age 52. Married for 28 years. A homemaker in Delhi. Your daughter in law Priya came to you two weeks ago and told you something about your son Ñ something about what happens at night in their room when she says no. You did not know what to say. You told her to be patient, that these things happen in marriages, that she needs to understand her husband's needs. Priya has not come back to you since. You are sitting alone in the kitchen tonight thinking about what Priya told you and what you said and whether what you said was right and whether what your son is doing has a name and whether that name changes anything and what you would lose if you admitted it does.
